Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files.   <title>Prompt Composition Flow in Bridge</title>
  <subtitle>Preamble built once at session creation, injected on first prompt only, composing 14+ sections</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>buildPreamble() in memory.ts constructs preamble from 14 sections: tool notes, environment block, memory block, dailylog, wiki index, document indexes, scripts, user profile, and agent disciplines</fact>
    <fact>Session creation (sessionManager.ts create()) builds preamble once and stores in session.memoryPreamble property</fact>
    <fact>First prompt in run-prompt.ts prepends preamble via preambleInjected flag check at line 380-382</fact>
    <fact>Preamble includes compressed facts via local LLM when available, falling back to raw tail facts</fact>
    <fact>Working-state restoration, session archive, dream suggestions, and relay tasks injected at session creation before preamble freeze</fact>
    <fact>Tool notes include 11 Telegram-specific tools: SEND_FILE, ASK buttons, SCHEDULE, SKILL_USED, GOAL_DONE, RESTART, STICKER, WIKI_QUERY, SKILL_PROPOSE, CONTINUE, and conditional RELAY_DELEGATE/SPECIALIST_PROXY/PARALLEL_DELEGATE</fact>
    <fact>Memory block splits recent facts into user-preference vs agent-notes tracks, with topic index for older facts using shard file counts</fact>
    <fact>Environment block provides ${MEMORY_DIR}, ${SKILL_DIR}, ${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}, ${USER_ID} variables for cross-machine portability</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The bridge's prompt composition follows a frozen-snapshot pattern. At session creation (sessionManager.ts), buildPreambleWithBreakdown() assembles 14+ sections into a single string stored in session.memoryPreamble. These sections include: (1) tool notes for 11 Telegram bridge commands, (2) environment variables for portable paths, (3) memory block with recent facts split by user-preference vs agent-notes, (4) topic index built from shard file line counts, (5) agent disciplines (7 compressed behavior rules), (6) wiki index, (7) document tree indexes, (8) available scripts, (9) user profile, plus restoration blocks for (10) working-state, (11) session archive, (12) dream suggestions, and (13) relay tasks. The first prompt in run-prompt.ts checks preambleInjected flag and prepends this frozen preamble to user text only once. Additional dynamic context (fusion perspectives, budget warnings) can be prepended at prompt time but the core preamble remains unchanged throughout the session. This approach keeps context stable across turns while avoiding repeated LLM calls to rebuild memory summaries.</narrative>
